questions.question: Should the new tests be added to BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests (which has the existing comment test and HTTP server setup) or to BayesMarketApiUnitTests (which calls server.route_request directly without HTTP)?
questions.why_it_matters: BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests already has the comment_post helper, HTTP server, and the one existing comment test. BayesMarketApiUnitTests uses direct server.route_request calls. The choice determines which helpers are available and whether tests go through HTTP auth/rate-limit middleware.

questions.question: For GET /v1/markets/{id}/comments tests, should we test via HTTP (BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests.request) or via direct server.route_request? GET does not require agent_id auth.
questions.why_it_matters: GET comments doesn't go through write-route auth, so it could be tested in either class. Testing via HTTP is more realistic but slower. The existing comment test is in the auth/rate-limit class, so GET tests could go there for co-location.

questions.question: For the idempotency conflict test, what exact payload field should differ to trigger a conflict? The normalization only keeps 'body', so sending a different 'body' with the same idempotencyKey should trigger the conflict path.
questions.why_it_matters: The conflict check compares normalized_payload (which is just {"body": ...}). We need to confirm that sending the same idempotencyKey+accountId+marketId but a different body text triggers the 409 idempotency_conflict response.

questions.question: Should tests for market-not-active rejection directly manipulate server.MARKETS[market_id]['status'] or use the resolve/close API endpoints to change market status?
questions.why_it_matters: Direct manipulation is simpler and more isolated. Existing tests already set server.MARKETS['m1']['status'] = 'closed' directly (line 157). Using API calls would be more realistic but adds dependencies.

assumptions.assumption: Tests should be added to BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests class since it has the comment_post helper, HTTP server, and the existing comment test.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to create helpers in a different test class or create a new dedicated class for comment tests.

assumptions.assumption: GET comments tests should use the HTTP request() method in BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests (no agent_id needed since GET is not a write route), posting comments first via comment_post to populate data.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to test via server.route_request directly in BayesMarketApiUnitTests instead.

assumptions.assumption: The GET comments endpoint returns 200 with {marketId, comments: [], pagination: {fromSeq, limit, returned, nextFromSeq}, meta: {timestamp}} structure, and comments are sorted by seq ascending.
assumptions.if_wrong: Response structure mismatch would cause assertion failures; would need to read the actual response format.

assumptions.assumption: For idempotency replay, the replayed response includes 'meta.replayed': True and returns status 201, matching the replay_comment_post_outcome function behavior.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to adjust assertions for the replay response format.

assumptions.assumption: For market-not-active rejection, directly setting server.MARKETS['m1']['status'] to a non-active value (e.g., 'closed') is acceptable, matching existing test patterns.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to use API endpoints to change market status instead.

assumptions.assumption: Validation error tests for POST should cover: missing accountId, missing body, empty body, body exceeding MAX_COMMENT_BODY_LENGTH (2000 chars), and invalid idempotencyKey (empty string).
assumptions.if_wrong: May need additional or fewer validation cases.

assumptions.assumption: GET comments pagination test should verify: default pagination (no params), fromSeq filtering, limit capping, nextFromSeq calculation (present when more comments exist, null when at end), and 404 for non-existent market.
assumptions.if_wrong: May need different pagination test scenarios.

assumptions.assumption: All POST comment tests need an agent_id header since comments are categorized as TRADE_WRITE_CATEGORY which requires X-Bayes-Agent-Id.
assumptions.if_wrong: Tests would fail with 401/403 auth errors if agent_id is needed but not provided.
